Internal Incident Report (JP-APPI/FIEA) — Kogane Techno Holdings Co., Ltd. — 2026-05-30
On 2026-05-28 our SOC detected anomalous access to the Board Secretariat portal hosting draft materials for a confidential domestic acquisition (tender-offer planning) of Shirasuna Logistics Co., Ltd., an undisclosed material fact under FIEA.
Named user cluster: Mr. Daichi Moriyama (born 1987-04-11, age 39), Security Analyst at Kogane Techno Holdings, Kitasora Ward HQ, employee code EM-4472, workstation asset KT-LP-1143 (Shirasagi X1), accessed /board/tob-2026/ at 21:43 JST.
Log context shows d.moriyama@kogane-th.co.jp, VPN session 7f43-kt-4481, private IP 10.14.55.82, and Set-Cookie: sid=ktb-9b22; these weak identifiers collectively link to the same individual in this matter.
A separate contractor ID ext-proc-77 (no named person) from Kawami satellite office, device KM-Tab-22 on Wi‑Fi SSID KT-Guest, attempted to download “valuation_target_shirasuna_v5.xlsx” and “draft_Board_minutes_2026-05-27.docx”.
Potentially exposed data included pre-announcement valuation models, draft timely-disclosure checklists for the local exchange, and masked payroll lines with My Number (個人番号) tokens rendered as ****-****-****; no unmasked My Number values were found.
Isolated weak identifiers observed elsewhere (not linked to a person): DOB 1979-05-19, role “contract analyst”, Aomaki satellite office, and device OS “iOS 17”; these fields alone are not sufficient to identify a living individual.
Ms. Ayaka Fujii, Board Secretariat Manager, confirmed that only six insiders had role-based access to the tender-offer workspace, and has extended the trading freeze to related employees in accordance with FIEA insider controls.
No securities trades in issuer or target shares were executed by employees during the event window; coordination with Hoshikawa Capital Securities KK confirms pre-clearance remains suspended.
Root cause analysis indicates credential stuffing against a reused password on ext-proc-77; Mr. Moriyama’s account shows normal behavior and no lateral movement beyond his role-based scope.
APPI considerations: internal employee codes and device identifiers constitute personal data when linkable to a living person; however, the incident did not disclose names, home addresses, or unmasked individual numbers to external parties.
Mitigation executed: forced password resets and MFA for all contractors, revocation of session sid=ktb-9b22, network segmentation of /board/tob-2026/, and redaction safeguards for any documents containing individual codes.
Reporting plan: notify the Personal Data Officer and prepare a voluntary notice to the national personal data supervisory authority if later discovery reveals exposure of unmasked individual numbers; monitor guidance on fair disclosure given the TOB planning status.
Benign content discovered during forensics (not PII/MNPI): a cafeteria wellness survey summarizing “ages 20s/30s/40s” and office bicycle parking rules.
For questions, contact incident@kogane-th.co.jp or the Board Secretariat hotline at 03-5555-0198 (internal).
